audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's reply to Zophar: sarcastic rebuke of friends' assumed wisdom, appeal to animals/earth/fish, God's sovereignty over breath and nations, and reversal of rulers/counselors.

verses.restored: Job answered and said:
Surely you are the people, and wisdom will die with you.
But I too have a heart like you; I do not fall below you. Who does not know such things as these?
I am a laughingstock to my companion, one who called to the Divine and was answered; a laughingstock, righteous and whole.
In the thought of one at ease there is contempt for calamity; it is ready for those whose foot slips.
The tents of destroyers are at ease, and those who provoke the Divine are secure, those who bring a god in their hand.
But ask the animals, and they will teach you; the birds of the heavens, and they will tell you.
Or speak to the earth, and it will teach you; the fish of the sea will recount to you.
Who among all these does not know that the hand of the Eternal has done this?
In his hand is the life of every living thing and the breath of all human flesh.
Does not the ear test words as the palate tastes food?
With the aged is wisdom, and length of days is understanding.
With him are wisdom and strength; counsel and understanding are his.
Look, he tears down, and it cannot be rebuilt; he shuts over a person, and it cannot be opened.
Look, he withholds waters and they dry up; he sends them out and they overturn the earth.
With him are strength and sound wisdom; the deceived and the deceiver are his.
He leads counselors away stripped, and judges he makes fools.
He loosens the bond of kings and binds a belt around their waist.
He leads priests away stripped and overthrows the enduring ones.
He removes speech from the trusted and takes away the discernment of elders.
He pours contempt on nobles and loosens the belt of the strong.
He uncovers deep things from darkness and brings deep shadow to light.
He makes nations great and destroys them; he spreads nations out and leads them away.
He removes the heart of the heads of the people of the earth and makes them wander in a wasteland where there is no way.
They grope in darkness without light, and he makes them stagger like a drunkard.
